channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: This Ryzen-powered NAS is barely bigger than a shoe box and can hold 11 SSDs and HDDs, delivering more than 500TB of storage
item.description: The Aoostar WTR Max is a powerhouse in a compact form, delivering Ryzen power while fitting into a NAS barely bigger than a shoebox.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Bogus report on tariff pause briefly lifted markets before White House denied it
item.description: A bogus rumor that President Donald Trump was considering a pause in tariffs briefly lifted markets Monday before the White House shot down the unfounded reports.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Apple has introduced 8 new emojis with iOS 18.4 - here they are, ranked from best to worst
item.description: Apple has released its 18.4 software update, which includes 8 new emojis. Here they are, ranked from best to worst.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Google NotebookLM cheat sheet: Get fast insights into your docs
item.description: When Google first launched the NotebookLM beta in late 2023, the company billed it as an experimental note-taking app with a generative AI assistant built in. You can still use it to create notes, but the app has evolved into something much more novel and powerful.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra could have a new camera lens but no magnets
item.description: The latest Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra rumor points to minimal changes to the main camera, and no changes to two other features.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Massive modernization: Tips for overhauling IT at scale
item.description: Nimesh Mehta likens decommissioning legacy systems to going on an archeological dig: There are systems that still have a lot of value; it’s just a matter of unearthing them, taking out what isn’t needed, and building new processes on top.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Zero-emissions hydrogen-electric jet disrupts conventional flights
item.description: Beyond Aero's BYA-1: Hydrogen-electric jet with zero emissions, reduced costs, 2030 launch. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses a potential game changer in the evolution of air travel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP rep says Russia and China are 'laughing at us' after NSA director firing
item.description: Rep. Don Bacon (R-Neb.), a moderate Republican, slammed President Trump’s decision to fire the director of the National Security Agency (NSA) and his top deputy after demands from far-right influencer Laura Loomer, saying Sunday that China and Russia are “laughing at us” after that decision.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Apple just made it much easier to get a handle on your inbox. Here’s how
item.description: Keeping our inboxes organized often feels like an overwhelming task. If you’re fortunate, yours contains only messages from people you wish to communicate with. Realistically, though, most are cluttered with newsletters, receipts, social media digests, and more.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Trump extends TikTok sale deadline again—this time by 75 days
item.description: President Donald Trump on Friday said is signing an executive order to keep TikTok running in the U.S. for another 75 days to give his administration more time to broker a deal to bring the social media platform under American ownership.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft employees protest at 50th anniversary party over Israel contract
item.description: A pro-Palestinian protest by Microsoft employees interrupted the company’s 50th anniversary celebration Friday, the latest backlash over the tech industry’s work to supply artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Nintendo delays Switch 2 preorders because of Trump’s tariffs
item.description: Nintendo is pushing back preorders for its upcoming Nintendo Switch 2 while it figures out the implications of President Donald Trump’s aggressive global tariffs. It’s unclear when preorders will actually begin. They were supposed to start Wednesday, ahead of a June 5 launch date.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: TikTok deal pulled after Trump tariff announcement
item.description: A finalized TikTok deal was pulled Thursday after President Trump announced massive new tariffs against China, a source familiar with the negotiations told The Hill. Trump was poised to sign an executive order approving a deal that would have seen TikTok’s U.S.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Meta fact-checking program to officially end Monday
item.description: Meta, the parent company of Facebook and Instagram, will officially end its fact-checking program Monday, a top company official said.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Trump to give TikTok another 75-day extension
item.description: President Trump said Friday that he plans to give TikTok another 75-day extension as his administration nears a deal to avert a ban on the popular social media platform.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: An AI avatar tried to argue a case before a New York court. The judges weren't having it
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took only seconds for the judges on a New York appeals court to realize that the man addressing them from a video screen — a person about to present an argument in a lawsuit — not only had no law degree, but didn't exist at all.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Reset Your Router Remotely: A Guide for Smart Home Owners
item.description: Learn how to reset your router remotely using a web browser, smartphone app, or smart plug. Stay connected and keep your smart home devices running smoothly even when you're away from home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI vs. pro gambler's $1 million March Madness bet coming down to Duke and Houston
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — The $1 million March Madness wager between a pro gambler and an artificial-intelligence site will be decided by the winner of the Duke-Houston game in the Final Four. The Vegas bookies project the gambler, who picked 5 1/2-point favorite Duke, should win.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Blissfully fast and simple JavaScript frameworks
item.description: Simple and fast. That’s all we want from our software tools. And meeting the requirements, of course. You can’t go without that. Also, we’d like them to be pleasant to use, with a good developer experience. It would be really great to have a friendly community.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Enterprises are getting worse at multicloud
item.description: In the initial years of multicloud adoption, enterprises took cautious, calculated steps to build and manage infrastructures that spanned multiple cloud providers. The objective was flexibility, performance optimization, and risk mitigation.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates reflects upon a 50-year-old computer code that reshaped technology
item.description: Even as he grows older, Microsoft founder Bill Gates still fondly remembers the catalytic computer code he wrote 50 years ago that opened up a new frontier in technology.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tariff war throws building of data centers into disarray
item.description: Enterprise IT leaders are facing a double-whammy of uncertainties complicating their data center building decisions: The ever-changing realities of genAI strategies, and the back-and-forth nature of the current tariff wars pushed by the United States. “This is obviously a fluid situation.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: A behind-the-scenes look at Nintendo Switch 2
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Nintendo's Switch 2 is bigger and better than its predecessor of eight years past as the video game developer pushes to bring its brand into “a new era" where social experiences are at the forefront.
item.dc:creator: RYAN KRYSKA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Potential Nvidia chip shortage looms as Chinese customers rush to beat US sales ban
item.description: The AI chip shortage could become even more dire as Chinese customers are purportedly looking to hoard Nvidia chips ahead of a proposed US sales ban.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Django 5.2 release touts automatic model importing
item.description: The latest version of the Python web framework features an automatic model-importing capability in the shell. This release also spells the end of support levels for previous version 5.x releases.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Making Python faster won’t be easy, but it’ll be worth it
item.description: Want to make a Python user grind their teeth? Just recite three words: Python is slow. In many of the ways that matter, it’s true.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump to hold a meeting on possible investors to buy TikTok with possible ban at stake
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — President Donald Trump will hold a Wednesday meeting with aides about possible investors who could buy a stake in TikTok, a deal that could potentially stop the social media site from being banned in the United States.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
